title: Canada Police Investigate After Shots Fired At US Consulate In Toronto | 10 News
description: Canadian authorities have increased security after shots were fired at the US consulate in Toronto in what police have described as a “national security incident.”  It’s believed two men in a white Honda approached the downtown Toronto building around 4:30am before exiting the vehicle and firing multiple shots at the consulate then driving away.  Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said the incident is “a reprehensible act of violence and attempt at intimidation.”  No injuries were reported. title: US Says ‘Most Intense’ Day Of Strikes Will Hit Iran As Tehran Rules Out Negotiation Talks | 10 News
description: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth says American and Israeli forces are carrying out the most intense day of strikes on Iran since the conflict began.  He says Wednesday’s offensive will involve the largest number of fighter jets, bombers and coordinated strikes targeting Iran’s missile systems and military infrastructure.  Hegseth says the operation aims to destroy Iran’s missile stockpiles and naval capability while permanently preventing Tehran from developing nuclear weapons.  Meanwhile, Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Tehran will not return to negotiations with the United States, describing past talks as a “bitter experience” and blaming the US and Israel for the broader instability, while insisting Iran is acting in self-defence. title: At Least Six People Dead After Bus Fire In Switzerland | 10 News
description: At least six people have been killed and five others injured after a bus caught fire in western Switzerland, in what police say may have been a deliberate act.  The fire broke out on a bus in the main street of the small town of Kerzers around 6:25pm on Tuesday.  Police spokesperson Frederic Papaux said an investigation is underway and, “We have evidence suggesting a deliberate act by someone who was on the bus.”  Join the conversation and follow us on: X: https://x.com/10NewsAU Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/10NewsAU/ Instagram: http://www.instagram.com/10NewsAU/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@10NewsAU LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/10newsau  10 News airs at 5pm, seven nights a week in your local market on Network 10. title: Two More Iranian Women’s Football Team Members Granted Asylum In Australia | 10 News
description: A further two members of the Iranian women's football team have been granted humanitarian visas after taking up an offer to stay in Australia.   Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke spoke earlier today and said the women were able to be separated from their handlers at Sydney Airport last night where each player had to make the difficult decision whether to start a new life or return to their families in Iran where they faced possible persecution.
